rxn_class_summary: Metal-free electrochemical oxidative difluoroethylation/cyclization of N-arylacrylamides and N-methacryloyl-N-alkylbenzamides with sodium difluoroalkylsulfinates (RCF2SO2Na) to construct difluoroalkylated oxindoles and isoquinoline-1,3-diones.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation (SET) of the sulfinate anion MeCF2SO2- to a sulfonyl radical, which extrudes SO2 to give the difluoroethyl radical; a second anodic oxidation aromatizes the cyclohexadienyl radical intermediate; cathode reduces protons to H2.

key_intermediates.name: tertiary alkyl radical B (after addition to acrylamide)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes MeCF2SO2- by SET to sulfonyl radical A, which loses SO2 to give •CF2Me; this carbon radical adds to the acrylamide alkene to form radical B, which cyclizes onto the aryl ring to give cyclohexadienyl radical C; C is oxidized at the anode (−e-, −H+) to rearomatize to product, while protons are reduced at the cathode to H2.
rate_determining_step: Anodic SET oxidation of MeCF2SO2Na to generate the difluoroethyl radical (MeCF2SO2Na oxidized at 0.72 V, more easily than the acrylamide at 1.35 V).

probe_reagents.name: BHT (2,6-di-tert-butyl-4-methylphenol)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction greatly inhibited; only trace product formed.

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction fully suppressed; DPE-CF2Me adduct 36 isolated in 42%, confirming free difluoroethyl radical.

probe_reagents.name: Hu's cathodic-reduction conditions (benzothiazolyl difluoroethyl sulfone, TBAOAc)
probe_reagents.observation: 0% of oxindole 1 — cathodic-reduction protocol does not deliver the cyclized product, supporting an anodic-oxidation pathway.

probe_reagents.name: difluoroethyl sulfone (replacing MeCF2SO2Na) under standard conditions
probe_reagents.observation: 0% product, confirming that the difluoroethyl radical is produced by SET oxidation of the sulfinate, not from the sulfone.

probe_reagents.name: controlled potential electrolysis at 0.73 V vs Ag/AgNO3
probe_reagents.observation: Product 1 obtained in 64% yield, confirming sulfinate oxidation at the anode initiates the reaction.

notes: CV shows MeCF2SO2Na oxidation peak at 0.72 V vs N-methyl-N-phenylmethacrylamide at 1.35 V, indicating sulfinate is preferentially oxidized at the anode. The method also extends to cyclopropyldifluoromethylsulfinate. No KIE or DFT was reported; mechanism is supported by radical trapping, CV, and controlled-potential electrolysis.
